channels.contract: No observation corruption; non-regression anchor only.
channels.name: Clean reference

channels.contract: Independent coordinate flips on a lawful binary or bipolar serialization.
guardrails: Equal raw p must not be interpreted as equal semantic damage across representations.
BCF participates only if a lawful coordinate serialization is explicitly frozen.
channels.name: Binary symmetric coordinate corruption

channels.contract: A known subset of coordinates or symbols is marked erased.
guardrails: Zero-fill must not be presented as native erasure decoding.
The erasure mask must be explicit when the decoder actually uses it.
channels.name: Coordinate erasure

channels.contract: Representation-specific symbol, block, or finite-field corruption channel.
guardrails: Compare only inside lawful native symbol families.
Do not build a universal ranking from one raw corruption percentage.
channels.name: Native symbol or block corruption

channels.contract: The semantic source tuple is changed before encoding; not a channel noise model.
channels.name: Semantic factor corruption

channels.contract: The observation is off-manifold or approximately produced rather than a corrupted valid codeword.
channels.name: Approximate perceptual observation

channels.contract: Multiple valid tuples are bundled or superposed.
channels.name: Superposition interference
notes: This is U2, not U1 noise.

external_internal_distinction.external_observation_corruption: clean representation -> external corruption channel -> decoder
external_internal_distinction.internal_algorithmic_stochasticity: clean or noisy observation -> decoder initialization or iteration noise

task_scope: U1 noisy single-product design only; U2 remains separate

u1_u2_separation.u1_contract: one clean or corrupted single bound tuple -> exactly one factor index per domain
u1_u2_separation.u2_contract: superposed tuples -> tuple set or histogram output
